title: Pure Technical Analysis Knowledge Base

trader_profile.philosophy: Quick trades, many trades, small targets, strict risk control

steps.name: Higher timeframe bias (H4/H1)
actions: Mark trend direction with 200 EMA slope and structure (HH/HL vs LH/LL)
Mark key S/R zones, previous day high/low (PDH/PDL), weekly open
Note if price is trending, ranging, or at a decision point

steps.name: Execution timeframe structure (M15/M5)
actions: Identify the active swing: last break of structure (BOS) or change of character (CHoCH)
Mark supply/demand zones and untested order blocks
Locate liquidity pools: equal highs/lows, session highs/lows

steps.name: Entry timeframe trigger (M1/M5)
actions: Wait for price to reach a marked zone - never chase
Confirm with candlestick trigger (engulfing, pin bar, inside-bar break) plus momentum (RSI/Stoch)
Enter only in the direction of the step-1 bias unless trading a confirmed range

steps.name: Trade management
actions: Stop loss beyond the zone / last swing, never at obvious equal highs-lows
Scalps: fixed 1:1 to 1:1.5 RR, take profit fast
Day trades: partial at 1R, trail rest behind M5/M15 swings
Max 1-2% account risk per trade, hard daily loss cap of 3-5%

universal_indicator_stack.rule: Indicators confirm, structure decides. Never enter on an indicator signal alone.

golden_rules: Trade the session that suits the instrument (see sessions_sast.json)
Synthetic indices (V50/V75/V100) ignore sessions - trade them on pure structure 24/7
News candles on forex/gold/BTC are not technical - stand aside 15 min before/after red-folder news
3 consecutive losses = stop for the day
If ATR is abnormally low, the market is coiling - wait for the break, do not scalp chop
